title: Mismatched dims for transformer-type vis in loss calculation when model's in_channels=out_channels
author: YongchengYAO
body:
In the training script `t5_transformer.py`,  we have
```python
 model_pred = model_pred.chunk(2, dim=1)[0]
```
[here](https://github.com/ShihaoZhaoZSH/LaVi-Bridge/blob/5a725f379f153991b36f307e099de01500f364c2/train/t5_transformer.py#L181) to match the dimensions of `model_pred` and `noise` in 

```python
# Optimization
loss = F.mse_loss(model_pred.float(), noise.float(), reduction="mean")   
```

The script is compatible with models where the number of output channels is twice the number of input channels, such as the model `PixArt-alpha/PixArt-XL-2-512x512` in the sample script. We can find the in and out channel dims [here](https://huggingface.co/PixArt-alpha/PixArt-XL-2-512x512/blob/main/transformer/config.json) 

For other models (such as [Efficient-Large-Model/SANA1.5_1.6B_1024px_diffusers](https://huggingface.co/Efficient-Large-Model/SANA1.5_1.6B_1024px_diffusers/blob/main/transformer/config.json)) where in_channels=out_channels, how to adjust the training script. Shall we just ignore this line?
```python
 model_pred = model_pred.chunk(2, dim=1)[0]
```

title: How to set VIS_REPLACE_MODULES | GEGLU not in PixArt-alpha/PixArt-XL-2-512x512
author: YongchengYAO
body:
Hi, I was using the function below to check the named modules of the model ` PixArt-alpha/PixArt-XL-2-512x512`

```python
# ...
vis = Transformer2DModel.from_pretrained(
            'PixArt-alpha/PixArt-XL-2-512x512', subfolder="transformer"
        )
# ...

# Add this after loading your model to inspect its architecture
def print_model_modules(model, prefix=""):
    """Print all module types in the model"""
    module_types = set()
    for name, module in model.named_modules():
        module_type = module.__class__.__name__
        module_types.add(module_type)
        if prefix:
            print(f"{prefix}.{name}: {module_type}")
    
    print(f"\nUnique module types found: {sorted(module_types)}")
    return module_types

# After loading your vis model
print("=== Visual Model Architecture ===")
vis_module_types = print_model_modules(vis, "vis")
```

The output is:
`
Unique module types found: ['AdaLayerNormSingle', 'Attention', 'BasicTransformerBlock', 'Conv2d', 'Dropout', 'FeedForward', 'GELU', 'LayerNorm', 'Linear', 'ModuleList', 'PatchEmbed', 'PixArtAlphaCombinedTimestepSizeEmbeddings', 'PixArtAlphaTextProjection', 'PixArtTransformer2DModel', 'SiLU', 'TimestepEmbedding', 'Timesteps']`


As I can not see `GEGLU` in the output and we have `VIS_REPLACE_MODULES = {"Attention", "GEGLU"}` in the script [here](https://github.com/ShihaoZhaoZSH/LaVi-Bridge/blob/5a725f379f153991b36f307e099de01500f364c2/train/t5_transformer.py#L81), I am wondering how to set `VIS_REPLACE_MODULES` for a new transformer model.

title: lora weight
author: tanshuai0219
body:
When I run the code:
`
TEXT_ENCODER_REPLACE_MODULES = {"LlamaAttention"}
tokenizer = LlamaTokenizer.from_pretrained(llama2_dir)
text_encoder = LlamaForCausalLM.from_pretrained(llama2_dir, torch_dtype=torch.float16).to(device)
tokenizer.pad_token = '[PAD]'
text_encoder.eval()
# tokenizer.model_max_length = 256

text_encoder_lora_params, _ = inject_trainable_lora_extended(
    text_encoder, 
    r=32, 
    target_replace_module=TEXT_ENCODER_REPLACE_MODULES,
    # loras=None,  # path to lora .pt
)
`

then, I print text_encoder_lora_params, and get "[]" a null dict.

title: Use CLIP as text encoder
author: Espere-1119-Song
body:
Thanks for your great contribution to the Community.

I found that the experiment that uses CLIP as text encoder has been conducted in the paper, but I didn't find the corresponding code. Will you release the CLIP version code? I wonder how to deal with the linear layer of the attention layer in CLIP text encoder. Because it seems that the linear layer of the attention layer in CLIP is `NonDynamicallyQuantizableLinear,` not normal `nn.Linear`.

title: Training and Fine-tuning hardware requirements
author: kubernetes-bad
body:
Exciting paper! Thank you for doing this research and publishing it.

Do you want to share some insight on what type of compute is required for training LaVi-Bridge?

Since you've used around 2M text-image pairs to train this, it sounds like you'd need a cluster of GPUs to train this from scratch (please correct me if I'm wrong!). Is finetuning the adapter and LoRAs something that can be performed on a smaller, domain-specific dataset? I would be curious to know what kind of compute that would require.

Thanks!
